rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS ASSIGNEE OF CITIBANK VS. NADINE BALSCHAK-BROWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS ASSIGNEE OF CITIBANK's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Notice Of Opposition Hearing required. If judgment debtor claims that the amount levied from her bank account includes paid earnings that are exempt from levy pursuant to Civil Procedure Code section 704.070, she should bring to the Court hearing three prior bank account statement showing withdrawals and deposits, and her last three paystubs. Judgment debtor identified Section 704.070 as one of the grounds for exemption on the Claim of Exemption form that she submitted to the levying officer, but hasn't provided any supporting documentation. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS ROSADO et al VS. TINA DIAMOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Carlos Rosado And Lissette Osorio'S Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Financial Condition Of Defendant Barney Diamos As An Individual And President/Owner Of Diamos Business Group
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2014, Line 3, Plaintiffs Carlos Rosado And Lissette Osorio's Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Financial Condition Of Defendant Barney Diamos As An Individual And President/Owner Of Diamos Business Group. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to permit discovery of defendants financial condition is denied. Defendants' right of financial privacy is constitutionally protected. Valley Bank of Nevada vs. Superior Court (1975) 15 C3d 652. Pre-trial financial discovery requires a Court finding of a substantial probability - defined as "very likely" or "strong likelihood" - that punitive damages will be awarded. Evidence on both sides is weighed by the Court. Jabro v. Superior Court (2002) 115 Cal. Rptr. 2d 843. Plaintiff's evidence regarding the unlawful detainer action and related events cannot be considered. See Civil Code 47(b) and Kerner v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County (2012) 206 Cal. App. 4th 84. The habitability allegations asserted by plaintiffs may support a punitive damages award at trial. But as presented and disputed in the motion, the Court does not find the "substantial probability" sufficient to defeat defendants' constitutional privacy protections. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, Motion For Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is denied without prejudice. The motion to compel responses to the written discovery is granted. If the plaintiff fails to serve verified responses the motion for terminating sanction may be renewed. Sanctions are awarded against the plaintiff in the sum of $360.00 which shall be paid within 35 days along with payment of the previous sanction award of $200. rulings.Case Title: HIN QIN XU BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM VS. TRANSIT AMERICA SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The court denies the request for judicial notice. The request asks the Court to take judicial notice of the "following facts" but then does not identify the specific, instead attaching three state/assembly bills. Even if the court were to grant the request for judicial notice, the cited documents do not conclusively demonstrate that defendant is not liable for the incident in the Second Amended Complaint. Defendant raises factual issues that a summary judgment motion or trial should resolve. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN NICHOLSON et al VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two And Req.'S For Documents, Set Two, Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two And Requests For Documents, Set Two, Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel a further response to financial document discovery is granted on the condition it execute the confidentiality agreement in the form proposed by the plaintiff. The plaintiff shall have 30 days following the Order herein to comply. The plaintiff may reassert objections to the discovery as none are ruled upon at this time. Following service of responses, the parties shall meet and confer in good faith to attempt to resolve any disputes and objections. To the extent documents are withheld on privilege grounds, plaintiff shall provide a privilege log. Sanctions in the sum of $1,250.00 are awarded to plaintiff against defendant and its counsel based on counsel's failure to meet and confer in good faith, which includes refusal to agree to plaintiff's confidentiality proposal once Moskowitz vs. Superior Court (1982) 137 Cal. App. 3d 313 was brought to his attention. As to that portion of defendant's motion to compel further responses to the 50 series Form Interrogatories, a hearing is required. A copy of the written contract which is the subject of the discovery shall be brought to the hearing. If defendant's counsel appears at the hearing by telephone, the contract may be transmitted to the Court in digital format to the email address below. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA WOODS VS. CBRE GROUP, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Arb
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 16, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT CBRE GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration Granted. Causes of action one through six shall be resolved through individual arbitration. The class claims are dismissed. Litigation of cause of action seven, the PAGA claim, shall be stayed pending the completion of arbitration. Plaintiff's offer letter contains a valid and enforceable arbitration agreement that mutually binds Plaintiff and Defendant, and Plaintiff agreed to be bound by her Offer Letter. The agreement is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act because the contract involves interstate commerce and does not contain a California choice of law provision. The agreement is not unconscionable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
